proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps (worse performance status) is associated with shorter pfs_months in this prostate cancer cohort.
Patients with mcrpc=1 (metastatic castration-resistant disease) have shorter pfs_months than those without mcrpc.
Patients with visceral_mets=1 have shorter pfs_months than those without visceral metastases.

analyses.code: import statsmodels.api as sm; X = sm.add_constant(df[['ecog_ps']].astype(float)); m = sm.OLS(df['pfs_months'], X).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Univariate OLS: each 1-point increase in ecog_ps corresponds to a 1.16-month decrease in pfs_months (beta=-1.161, p<1e-300). Strongly significant in the expected direction.

Mean pfs_months = 3.51 in mcrpc=1 vs 4.03 in mcrpc=0 (t-test diff=-0.521, p=9.83e-182). Strongly significant; mCRPC patients have ~0.5-month shorter PFS univariately, though this attenuates after multivariable adjustment (see iter 18).
Mean pfs_months = 3.706 with visceral_mets vs 3.754 without (diff=-0.048, p=0.0352). Direction matches expectation but the univariate effect is small.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher log(1+psa_ng_ml) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher gleason_score is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Older age_years is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Each 1-unit increase in log(1+PSA) corresponds to a 0.382-month decrease in pfs_months (beta=-0.382, p~0). PSA on the log scale is one of the strongest univariate predictors.
Beta=-0.0062 per 1-point Gleason increase, p=0.486. NOT significant; gleason_score does not have a detectable univariate association with pfs_months in this dataset.
Beta=+0.174 months per year of age (p<1e-300). DIRECTION OPPOSITE to expectation: older patients have LONGER PFS. Refutes the hypothesized negative direction; sign is positive and highly significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (a positive prognostic marker).
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher alkaline_phosphatase_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (reflecting bone/liver involvement).

analyses.result_summary: Beta=+0.495 months per 1 g/dL of albumin (p=1.5e-166). Strongly significant in expected direction: higher albumin is favorable.
Beta=-0.00042 months per U/L of LDH (p=0.000181). Highly significant; higher LDH worsens PFS, as expected, though absolute effect per unit is small.
Beta=-0.00050 months per U/L of ALP (p=0.0066). Significant in expected direction but effect attenuates and becomes NS after multivariable adjustment (see iter 18).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher crp_mg_l (inflammation) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher nlr (neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Beta=-0.0749 months per 1 percentage-point of weight loss (p=9.9e-226). Highly significant; cachexia/weight loss strongly worsens PFS.
Beta=-0.00064 per mg/L CRP, p=0.507. NOT significant; CRP shows no detectable association with pfs_months.
Beta=+0.0024 per unit NLR, p=0.577. NOT significant; NLR is not associated with PFS in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have longer pfs_months than those not receiving it (overall main effect).
Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have longer pfs_months than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_lu177_psma have longer pfs_months than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_enzalutamide have different pfs_months than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_abiraterone have different pfs_months than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_docetaxel have different pfs_months than those not receiving it.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months = 3.83 on olaparib vs 3.74 off (diff=+0.094, p=0.00162). Significant positive main effect, but driven entirely by BRCA2-mutated subgroup (see iter 6).
Mean pfs_months = 3.81 on pembrolizumab vs 3.74 off (diff=+0.064, p=0.133). NOT significant overall.
Mean pfs_months = 3.74 on Lu-177-PSMA vs 3.74 off (diff=-0.004, p=0.872). NOT significant; no overall benefit detected.
Mean pfs_months = 3.73 on enzalutamide vs 3.76 off (diff=-0.026, p=0.154). NOT significant.
Mean pfs_months = 3.74 on abiraterone vs 3.75 off (diff=-0.006, p=0.776). NOT significant.
Mean pfs_months = 3.73 on docetaxel vs 3.75 off (diff=-0.017, p=0.382). NOT significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive interaction between treatment_olaparib and brca2_mutation: olaparib improves pfs_months substantially in brca2_mutation=1 patients but not (or much less) in brca2_mutation=0 patients (synthetic lethality).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction term beta=+1.619 (p=4.33e-61). Stratified: in brca2_mutation=1 (n=4996), olaparib increases pfs_months by +1.55 months (p=9.1e-54); in brca2_mutation=0 (n=45004), olaparib gives -0.073 months (p=0.019). MASSIVE positive synthetic-lethality interaction confirmed.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive interaction between treatment_pembrolizumab and msi_high: pembrolizumab benefits MSI-high patients more than MSI-low patients.
There is a positive interaction between treatment_lu177_psma and psma_high: Lu-177-PSMA benefits PSMA-high patients more than PSMA-low patients.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta=-0.340, p=0.153 (NOT significant). Stratified: in msi_high=1 (n_treat=79, n_ctrl=1449), pembrolizumab vs no pembrolizumab gives diff=-0.27 months (p=0.27). The hypothesized positive MSI/pembrolizumab interaction is NOT detected; sign is actually negative and underpowered.

Interaction beta=-0.017, p=0.737 (NOT significant). Stratified: in psma_high=1, Lu-177 effect is -0.011 (p=0.74); in psma_high=0, Lu-177 effect is +0.006 (p=0.87). No PSMA-specific benefit of Lu-177-PSMA is detectable.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with ar_v7_positive=1 have a smaller benefit (or relative harm) from treatment_enzalutamide compared with ar_v7_positive=0 patients (a negative interaction reflecting AR-V7 resistance).
Patients with ar_v7_positive=1 have a smaller benefit (or relative harm) from treatment_abiraterone compared with ar_v7_positive=0 patients.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta=+0.0025, p=0.957 (NOT significant). Stratified: in ar_v7_positive=1, enzalutamide diff=-0.024 (p=0.56); in ar_v7_positive=0, enzalutamide diff=-0.027 (p=0.19). NO detectable AR-V7-modified enzalutamide effect.
Interaction beta=+0.048, p=0.334 (NOT significant). NO detectable AR-V7-modified abiraterone effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: tp53_mutation status is associated with shorter pfs_months (poor prognosis).
pten_loss is associated with shorter pfs_months.
There is a negative interaction between treatment_docetaxel and tp53_mutation (p53-deficient tumors respond less well to docetaxel).

analyses.result_summary: Beta=+0.0031, p=0.867. NOT significant; tp53_mutation is not associated with pfs_months in this cohort.
Beta=+0.021, p=0.616. NOT significant; pten_loss not associated with PFS.
Interaction beta=+0.046, p=0.247 (NOT significant). No tp53-modified docetaxel effect detected.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Major comorbidities (chronic_kidney_disease, heart_failure, coronary_artery_disease, atrial_fibrillation) are each associated with shorter pfs_months.
diabetes_mellitus and hypertension are each associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: All four NOT significant: CKD beta=+0.033 (p=0.23); heart_failure beta=-0.003 (p=0.92); CAD beta=+0.0001 (p=0.99); afib beta=-0.037 (p=0.28). Hypothesis NOT supported.
Both NOT significant: diabetes beta=-0.029 (p=0.19); hypertension beta=-0.024 (p=0.18). Direction is consistent with hypothesis but neither reaches significance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: liver_mets is associated with shorter pfs_months relative to no liver_mets.
bone_mets is associated with shorter pfs_months.
pleural_effusion and pericardial_effusion are each associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Beta=-0.0017, p=0.947. NOT significant; surprisingly, liver_mets has no detectable PFS effect in this cohort.
Beta=+0.011, p=0.624. NOT significant; bone_mets has no detectable independent PFS effect (and direction is positive, opposite expectation).
pleural_effusion beta=-0.043 (p=0.088); pericardial_effusion beta=-0.036 (p=0.435). Neither significant; pleural_effusion is borderline.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher fatigue_grade, pain_nrs, dyspnea_grade, cough_grade, and appetite_loss_grade are each associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.code: Five univariate OLS on each symptom score
analyses.result_summary: All five NOT significant: fatigue_grade beta=+0.004 (p=0.66); pain_nrs beta=-0.001 (p=0.80); dyspnea_grade beta=-0.002 (p=0.81); cough_grade beta=+0.002 (p=0.79); appetite_loss_grade beta=-0.001 (p=0.91). Patient-reported symptom grades have no detectable PFS association in this cohort, in contrast to weight_loss_pct_6mo which is highly significant (h10).

proposed_hypotheses.text: race_ethnicity is associated with pfs_months: black, hispanic, asian, or other patients differ from white patients.
insurance_type is associated with pfs_months: medicare, medicaid, or uninsured differ from privately insured patients.
rural_residence, smoking_pack_years, or education_years are each associated with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: asian-white diff=+0.056 (p=0.145); black-white diff=-0.0005 (p=0.99); hispanic-white diff=-0.004 (p=0.89); other-white diff=+0.008 (p=0.91). NONE significant. Hypothesis NOT supported; no race/ethnicity differences in PFS.
medicare-private diff=+0.018 (p=0.36); medicaid-private diff=+0.005 (p=0.87); uninsured-private diff=-0.010 (p=0.83). NONE significant. Hypothesis NOT supported.
rural_residence beta=+0.015 (p=0.50); smoking_pack_years beta=+0.0002 (p=0.51); education_years beta=-0.003 (p=0.40). NONE significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 25 SNP loci profiled, none has a real main effect on pfs_months; the number of nominal p<0.05 hits should be near the chance expectation of ~1.25 / 25.
snp_rs4986893 (CYP2C19*3 loss-of-function) is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.code: Loop OLS over snp_* columns; count p<0.05
analyses.result_summary: 1 of 25 SNPs (snp_rs4986893) reached nominal p<0.05; expected by chance ~1.25. Consistent with a global null for SNP main effects.

Beta=-0.092, p=0.0145. Direction is negative as hypothesized, p<0.05 nominally, but does not survive multiple-testing correction (Bonferroni alpha=0.002 over 25 SNPs). Likely a chance hit; not robust.

proposed_hypotheses.text: her2_amplification, met_exon14_skipping, ret_fusion, ros1_fusion, braf_v600e, ntrk_fusion, nrg1_fusion, fgfr_alteration, cdkn2a_loss, keap1_mutation, and pik3ca_mutation are each associated with pfs_months.

analyses.code: Univariate OLS on each non-prostate molecular alteration
analyses.result_summary: ALL NS: HER2 amp p=0.71; MET ex14 p=1.00; RET p=0.50; ROS1 p=0.60; BRAF V600E p=0.91; NTRK p=0.16; NRG1 p=0.45; FGFR p=0.54; CDKN2A loss p=0.10; KEAP1 p=0.36; PIK3CA p=0.67. None significant. Hypothesis NOT supported - these (largely lung/colon-style) drivers are unlinked to PFS in this prostate cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
Platelet count, WBC, ANC, and ALC each have detectable univariate associations with pfs_months.
Higher creatinine_mg_dl, bun_mg_dl, or calcium_mg_dl is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Beta=-0.0115 per g/dL, p=0.0225. Marginally significant in OPPOSITE direction (slight negative slope - hemoglobin is NOT a positive prognostic marker here). Effect disappears entirely (p=0.92) in the adjusted multivariable model.

analyses.code: Four univariate OLS on CBC components
analyses.result_summary: ALL NS: platelets p=0.83; WBC p=0.34; ANC p=0.43; ALC p=0.50. CBC components have no detectable PFS association.

creatinine beta=-0.044 (p=0.144); BUN beta=-0.0016 (p=0.37); calcium beta=-0.028 (p=0.12). None significant; direction (negative) consistent with hypothesis for two of three but not statistically supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Vital signs (systolic_bp_mmhg, diastolic_bp_mmhg, heart_rate_bpm, spo2_pct) and bmi are each associated with pfs_months.
Liver-function tests ast_u_l, alt_u_l, total_bilirubin_mg_dl have detectable univariate associations with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: SBP beta=+0.0011 (p=0.079); DBP p=0.39; HR p=0.87; SpO2 p=0.32; BMI p=0.13. None significant. Hypothesis NOT supported.
AST p=0.52; ALT p=0.61; total_bilirubin beta=-0.062 (p=0.0315). Bilirubin is the only one nominally significant, in expected direction, but does not survive multiple-testing correction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable OLS adjusting for age_years, ecog_ps, mcrpc, visceral_mets, log_psa, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, weight_loss_pct_6mo, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, and hemoglobin_g_dl, ecog_ps remains a strong negative predictor and log_psa, albumin, LDH, and weight_loss remain significant in their expected directions, while mcrpc, hemoglobin, and ALP attenuate to non-significance.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted betas (all p<1e-50 unless noted): age_years=+0.174, ecog_ps=-1.150, log_psa=-0.383, albumin=+0.478, ldh=-0.00052, weight_loss_pct_6mo=-0.077, visceral_mets=-0.016 (p=4.9e-5). Attenuated to NS: mcrpc beta=+0.0007 (p=0.85), hemoglobin beta=-0.0001 (p=0.92), ALP beta=-3.5e-5 (p=0.27). Hypothesis SUPPORTED on all counts. R^2=0.97 indicates the listed covariates capture ~97% of pfs_months variance, leaving little residual signal for other features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Adding treatment_olaparib, brca2_mutation, and their interaction to the multivariable model from h44 leaves the interaction term highly significant and large (~+1.4 months in BRCA2-mutated patients), while olaparib and BRCA2 main effects collapse to ~0.

analyses.code: Multivariable OLS with treatment_olaparib + brca2_mutation + olaparib*brca2
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted: olaparib main beta=-0.0001 (p=0.98); BRCA2 main beta=+0.0003 (p=0.96); olaparib*BRCA2 interaction beta=+1.433 (p<1e-300). Model R^2 rises to 0.975. Hypothesis SUPPORTED: the only treatment signal is the synthetic-lethality interaction in BRCA2-mutated patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: ECOG performance status modifies treatment effect: at least one of the six treatments has a significantly different effect across ECOG levels (ecog_ps x treatment interaction).

analyses.code: Six OLS models with treatment*ecog interaction term
analyses.result_summary: ALL six interactions NOT significant: enzalutamide*ecog beta=-0.026 (p=0.29); abiraterone*ecog p=0.65; docetaxel*ecog p=0.66; olaparib*ecog beta=-0.037 (p=0.36); lu177_psma*ecog p=0.47; pembrolizumab*ecog p=0.48. Hypothesis NOT supported - no treatment-by-ECOG heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with visceral_mets benefit relatively more from treatment_docetaxel than non-visceral patients (positive interaction).
Patients with bone_mets benefit relatively more from treatment_lu177_psma than non-bone-mets patients (positive interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta=+0.052, p=0.294 (NOT significant). Direction matches hypothesis but signal is undetectable.
Interaction beta=-0.054, p=0.393 (NOT significant). Direction OPPOSITE to hypothesis (smaller benefit in bone_mets) but not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: prior_chemotherapy, prior_radiation, prior_surgery, prior_immunotherapy, prior_targeted_therapy, prior_lines_of_therapy, and years_since_diagnosis are each associated with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: ALL NS: prior_chemo p=0.59; prior_RT p=0.71; prior_surgery beta=-0.030 (p=0.12); prior_immuno p=0.39; prior_targeted p=0.46; prior_lines beta=-0.010 (p=0.20); years_since_diagnosis p=0.90. None significant; treatment history does not predict PFS in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 14 chronic medical comorbidity flags (diabetes_mellitus through interstitial_lung_disease_history), depression_anxiety_diagnosis is associated with shorter pfs_months.
hiv_positive is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Beta=-0.045, p=0.059. Borderline; direction matches hypothesis but does not reach alpha=0.05.
Beta=-0.033, p=0.722. NOT significant. Hypothesis NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: ca_125_u_ml, cea_ng_ml, tsh_uiu_ml, inr, sodium_meq_l, potassium_meq_l, glucose_mg_dl have detectable univariate associations with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Mostly NS: CA-125 p=0.18; CEA p=0.37; TSH p=0.48; INR p=0.52; sodium p=0.76; potassium p=0.14; glucose beta=+0.00071 (p=0.032). Only glucose reaches nominal p<0.05 with a tiny positive coefficient; will not survive multiple testing.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Refined summary: pfs_months in this cohort is a near-deterministic function of (1) age_years (positive), (2) ecog_ps (negative, large), (3) log(1+psa_ng_ml) (negative, large), (4) albumin_g_dl (positive, large), (5) ldh_u_l (negative, small), (6) weight_loss_pct_6mo (negative, large), (7) visceral_mets (negative, small), and (8) treatment_olaparib x brca2_mutation interaction (positive, very large; ~+1.4 months in BRCA2-mutated patients only). All other features (treatments alone, biomarker-treatment interactions other than olaparib*BRCA2, comorbidities, demographics, mets sites, prior therapies, symptom scores other than weight loss, vitals, CBC, lipids/electrolytes, SNPs, and non-prostate molecular drivers) have no detectable independent effect on PFS.

analyses.code: Final multivariable OLS with all 8 prognostic features + olaparib*BRCA2 interaction
analyses.result_summary: Final model R^2=0.975. Coefficients (all p<1e-50): age_years=+0.174, ecog_ps=-1.151, log_psa=-0.383, albumin_g_dl=+0.478, ldh_u_l=-0.00052, weight_loss_pct_6mo=-0.077, visceral_mets=-0.015 (p=4.9e-5), olaparib*BRCA2=+1.433. The eight-term model captures essentially all explainable variance in pfs_months; residual variance is consistent with simulation/measurement noise. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.
